title: anthriscus vulgaris var. hispanica
intro: Anthriscus vulgaris var. hispanica, also known as wild celery or cow parsley, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve flatulence, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of wild celery include its potential to reduce blood pressure, lower cholesterol levels, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Wild celery is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and it can also be used in soups and salads, although it is essential to note that the plant's similarity to hemlock can make it easily mistaken for a toxic lookalike.
